COBOL Embedded SQL: Table Join Example.prefix: COBOL Embedded SQL: Table Join Example
body: DISPLAY '**** E R R O R M E S S A G E S               ****'.
** Note - the following statement will end the program
COBOL Embedded SQL: Table Join Example.description:
Embedded DB2 Select/From/Where (table join) statement with fictitious Table & Column names. 
Note that the  "Join Condition" **typically** matches (compares) the Primary Key of one table with with the Foreign Key of another table.

COBOL Embedded SQL: Subquery (Sub-Select.prefix: COBOL Embedded SQL: Subquery (Sub-Select IN) Example
body: DISPLAY '**** E R R O R M E S S A G E S               ****'.
** Note - the following statement will end the program
COBOL Embedded SQL: Subquery (Sub-Select.description:
Embedded DB2 Select/From/Where (table join) statement with fictitious Table & Column names. 
Note that the  "Subquery" **typically** selects compares the Primary Key of the table referenced in the outer select, with with the Foreign Key of  table referenced in the sub-select. However, note that there are lots of other processing scenarios and use cases. This example uses the IN operator - which roughly translates to "equal any" ... in other words, return a row through the Outer Select where the column value equals any value returned by the sub-select.

COBOL Embedded SQL: Update Where Current.prefix: COBOL Embedded SQL: Update Where Current of Cursor
body: *** This code can be placed in Working-Storage   ***
***  Copy an interactive SQL Statement between   ***
***  EXEC SQL and END-EXEC.  Modify any host     ***
***  Note that FOR UPDATE OF <column> must       ***
***    be specified at the end of the Select     ***
***    for every column that is to be updated    ***
*** This code must be placed in the Procedure Division ***
****  and must be executed before a Fetch statement    ***
If SQLCODE Not Equal to Zero
** This code must be placed in the Procedure Division  ***
** It must be executed AFTER the Cursor Open statement ***
** and is typically executed in a Loop.                ***
** Note that if the table columns contain underscores  ***
**    you will need to change them to dashes in COBOL  ***
** Copy select columns expression here, and prefix     ***
If SQLCODE Not Equal to (Zero or +100)
*** This code must be placed in the Procedure Division ***
****  and should be executed after you're finished     ***
****  processing all of the cursor result rows         ***
If SQLCODE Not Equal to Zero
DISPLAY '**** E R R O R M E S S A G E S               ****'.
** Note - the following statement will end the program
COBOL Embedded SQL: Update Where Current.description: When your program has positioned the cursor on a row, you can update the row by using the UPDATE statement with the WHERE CURRENT OF clause. The WHERE CURRENT OF clause specifies a cursor that points to the row that you want to update.

COBOL Embedded SQL: Delete Where Current.description: When your program has retrieved the current row, you can delete the row by using the DELETE statement with the WHERE CURRENT OF clause. The WHERE CURRENT OF clause specifies a cursor that points to the row that you want to delete.

COBOL Embedded SQL: Cursor Declare State.prefix: COBOL Embedded SQL: Cursor Declare Statement
body: *** This code can be placed in Working-Storage   ***
***  Copy an interactive SQL Statement between   ***
***  EXEC SQL and END-EXEC.  Modify any host     ***
COBOL Embedded SQL: Cursor Declare State.description: The DECLARE CURSOR statement names a cursor and specifies a select-statement. The select-statement defines a set of rows that, conceptually, make up the result table.  Note that Declare Cursor is not operational - and can be coded in WORKING-STORAGE or in the PROCEDURE DIVISION.

body: * Processing is dependent upon the key that was pressed and
* field EIBAID contains this information.  EIBAID is set to
* dfhpf3 when function key 3 has been pressed, DFHCLEAR for
* the clear/pause key and DFHENTER for the enter key.
* key testing is necessary to determine the action required.

COBOL Snippet: Procedure Division - for.prefix: COBOL Snippet: Procedure Division Snippet - for Batch COBOL
body: *  This routine should perform file open and an initial read
*  Code your statement here to OPEN files
*  Code your statement here to read the input file
*  Remember to move "NO" to IFCODE if the input file is AT END
*  This routine contains the business logic for the program
*  Validate patient type and insurance coverage
*  Add to counters and total amounts
*  Move input data to output data
*  Code your statements here to read the input file
*  Remember to move "NO" to IFCODE if the input file is AT END
*  This routine should perform file close operations
*  Code the statement write the final output record
*  Code the statement to close all files
*  Code the statement to Display a successful end-of-job message
